concerns.text: Three alpha-diversity metrics (Shannon, Simpson, Faith's PD) are computed from the same OTU table and are mathematically correlated. Using them as separate predictors without addressing collinearity violates predictor independence assumptions. FR-012 mentions diagnosing collinearity but does not specify how correlated diversity metrics will be handled in model specification.

ANCOM stratifies samples into high/low disease groups using disease incidence, which is also the outcome variable in the main beta regression/GLMM model. While this is standard exploratory practice, the plan does not clearly distinguish between confirmatory (diversity-disease relationship) and exploratory (taxon-level) analyses, risking circular interpretation.

Permutation test specification does not indicate whether stratification (by plant species, geographic region) will be maintained during permutation. Unstratified permutations may violate the hierarchical data structure assumed in the GLMM, affecting null distribution validity.

Power analysis specifies effect size ≥0.1 without literature justification. This arbitrary threshold may lead to underpowered or overpowered study design. Effect size should be derived from published soil microbiome-disease meta-analyses per SC-001.

SC-001 requires comparing correlation coefficients against 'published meta-analysis values from soil microbiome-disease literature' but does not specify which meta-analysis. If no such meta-analysis exists or values vary widely, this success criterion cannot be objectively measured.
